articles.title: Alarm sirens in Bahran sound again, residents urged to 'remain calm'
articles.summary: Alarm sirens in Bahran sound again, residents urged to 'remain calm' Alarm sirens are sounding again in Bahrain, amid a fresh missile barrage fired from Iran. The country's Interior Ministry is urging residents to “to remain calm and head to the nearest safe place". This is the fourth time alarms have sounded  in the Gulf kingdom in the last 24 hours, as Iran has fired several barrages of missiles and drones throughout Tuesday. Iran's IRGC claimed attacks on infrastructure belonging to US company Amazon and a military base.

Bahrain downs incoming Iranian missiles
articles.summary: Bahrain downs incoming Iranian missiles Bahrain's Defence Forces have said their air defences downed a barrage of missiles fired from Iran. In a statement, it urged citizens “to be cautious, not to approach any strange or suspicious objects resulting from the residues of the heinous Iranian attack, and report them immediately”. The General Command of Bahrain’s defence force stated that “the deliberate use of missiles and marching aircraft to target civilians and private property is a blatant violation of International Humanitarian Law”.
